preview_text:
The model rates France as a strong favorite, assigning them a 59% win probability against a Norway side given an 15% chance to upset on a neutral ground. The remaining probability concentrates on a draw, which the model estimates at 27%.

The win-loss gap of roughly 44 percentage points reflects an asymmetric matchup where draws carry material weight. A draw remains plausible at better than one-in-four odds, suggesting the model sees meaningful defensive organization from Norway despite France's overall superiority. The spread indicates a competitive contest in execution rather than an open affair.

The model and sportsbook consensus align closely across all three outcomes. France's advantage narrows from 59% to 55% in the betting market, a 4-point shift, while Norway gains 4 points to 19%. The draw estimate is nearly identical. This convergence suggests broad agreement on the match's character. No Polymarket data is available at this stage.
